key_insight: A non-overlapping domain decomposition framework turns the coupled Stokes-Biot fluid–poroelastic problem into an interface system solved efficiently with Lagrange multipliers and GMRES.
engineering_relevance: This enables parallel, scalable simulation of fluid–structure interaction in poroelastic media, which is important for biomedical, geomechanics, and multiphysics engineering applications.
method: Split the domain into Stokes and Biot subdomains with Stokes-Stokes, Biot-Biot, and Stokes-Biot interfaces.
Enforce interface physics weakly using Lagrange multipliers for velocity, displacement, and pressure continuity/stress balance.
Reduce the global mixed FEM system via Schur complement to an interface problem and solve it with GMRES in parallel.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments and a field-of-value analysis showing the interface operator is positive definite and supporting GMRES convergence.
watch_item: Performance depends on stable mixed finite element choices and accurate treatment of multiple interface multipliers, which can increase implementation complexity.
paper_title: Domain decomposition methods for the Stokes-Biot model of fluid-poroelastic structure interaction
